instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘Asubhāya cittaṃ bhāvehi, ekaggaṃ susamāhitaṃ; Sati kāyagatā tyatthu, nibbidābahulo bhava.

instances.object: rūpa-kammaṭṭhāna (form, by the Buddha) + ānāpānassati (by the elder Sāriputta)
instances.recipient: Rāhula (the venerable Rāhula / Rāhulabhadda)
instances.recipient_features: left behind in the monastery; the Buddha taught him the form-subject and his preceptor Sāriputta breath-mindfulness; obedient pupil who reflected on his teachers' instruction
instances.occasion: Mahārāhulovāda setting; both teachers having declared a kammaṭṭhāna to him departed, and Rāhula remained, applying the assigned rūpa-subject as anicca/dukkha/asubha/anattā
instances.warrant: MN 62 Mahārāhulovāda-sutta (the Buddha teaches Rāhula form/the elements; Sāriputta recommends ānāpānassati)
instances.evidence_pali: Iti bhagavā rūpakammaṭṭhānaṃ, thero ānāpānassatinti ubhopi kammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitvā gatā, rāhulabhaddo vihāreyeva ohīno.

instances.object: unstated (unspecified kammaṭṭhāna given)
instances.recipient: a group of monks sent by the Tissamahāvihāra bhikkhu-saṅgha to summon Elder Dhammadinna
instances.recipient_features: monks of the saṅgha sent to fetch the elder; on answering that they 'seek the self' they are called sappurisā (true men)
instances.occasion: the saṅgha repeatedly sent monks to summon Dhammadinna; he tested them ('do you seek the self or another?') and on their reply gave them a meditation subject, after which all attained arahatship
instances.warrant: none (commentarial narrative; generic kammaṭṭhāna-giving frame, no specific canonical antidote formula)
instances.evidence_pali: so tesaṃ kammaṭṭhānamadāsi, sabbeva arahattaṃ pāpuṇiṃsu

instances.object: unstated (unspecified kammaṭṭhāna given)
instances.recipient: a sixty-rains elder (saṭṭhivassātīta mahāthera) of the Haṅkanavihāra who falsely claimed arahantship through conceit (adhimāna)
instances.recipient_features: ordained over sixty years; overcome by adhimāna, wrongly believing himself an arahant; realized 'I am not an arahant' after his own conjured elephant frightened him
instances.occasion: after Dhammadinna exposed the false conceit via the iddhi-conjured elephant, he consoled the elder ('over-estimation arises only in those still doing the work') and gave him a meditation subject; the elder then attained arahatship
instances.warrant: none (commentarial narrative of consolation-then-assignment to an elder; no specific canonical antidote formula cited)
instances.evidence_pali: Dhammadinno ‘‘mā, bhante, soci, mā anattamano ahosi, kārakānaṃyeva adhimāno uppajjatī’’ti theraṃ samassāsetvā kammaṭṭhānamadāsi. Thero tassovāde ṭhatvā arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.

instances.object: asubha
instances.recipient: a young monk (daharabhikkhu) led by the novice (sāmaṇera) to the spot of a foul object
instances.recipient_features: junior monk who, directed to the same spot and to look facing east, attained the very distinction the novice had reached
instances.occasion: the novice had attained jhāna and three fruits from an asubha, then led the daharo to the same place to look so he too attained; illustrates sappāyasampajañña (a man's foul object differs in suitability from a woman's)
instances.warrant: the asubha / paṭikūlamanasikāra section of the Satipaṭṭhāna (MN 10 / DN 22); carita-suitability matrix in Vism (asubha for rāgacarita)
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘bhante, iminā tāva maggena gantvā mayā ṭhitokāse muhuttaṃ puratthābhimukho ṭhatvā olokethā’’ti āha. So tathā katvā tena pattavisesameva pāpuṇi.

instances.object: tejodhātu-kasiṇa (tejodhātukasiṇaparikamma — fire-element kasiṇa preliminary)
instances.recipient: Tissa-sāmaṇera (the novice), pupil of an elder at Tissamahāvihāra
instances.recipient_features: discontented novice ('bhante ukkaṇṭhitomhi'); his preceptor relocated him to Cittalapabbata and set him a private dwelling-task
instances.occasion: on the novice's declared discontent the upajjhāya moved him to Cittalapabbata; the novice undertook Saṃyutta recitation, slope-clearing, and the fire-element-kasiṇa preliminary together, brought the kammaṭṭhāna to absorption, then developed insight to arahatship
instances.warrant: none specific to this narrative (the kasiṇa scheme itself is in the Vism kasiṇa-bhāvanā list, but the directed assignment to this novice is commentarial vatthu)
instances.evidence_pali: saṃyuttanikāyaṃ pabbhārasodhanaṃ tejodhātukasiṇaparikammanti tīṇīpi ekato ārabhitvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ appanaṃ pāpesi

instances.object: unstated (unspecified kammaṭṭhāna given)
instances.recipient: Upaka the Ājīvaka (named individual), newly ordained by the Buddha
instances.recipient_features: from Vaṅkahāra country; ordained late ('mahallako jāto'); the man who had earlier met the Buddha on the road asking about 'Anantajina'; later reached the non-returner fruit, was reborn in Aviha, and attained arahatship there
instances.occasion: Upaka sought out the Buddha at Sāvatthī, was ordained by him, and the Buddha gave him a meditation subject
instances.warrant: none specific (commentarial expansion of the Upaka episode of MN 26 Pāsarāsi / Ariyapariyesanā; canon does not record the assignment, and no criterion-based antidote is stated)
instances.evidence_pali: Bhagavā pabbājetvā tassa kammaṭṭhānaṃ adāsi. So kammaṭṭhāne kammaṃ karonto anāgāmiphale patiṭṭhāya

instances.object: unstated (the Buddha clears defects in the five monks' existing kammaṭṭhāna; the Anattalakkhaṇa-sutta is then taught to lead them to arahatship)
instances.recipient: the five monks (pañcavaggiyā bhikkhū): Vappa, Bhaddiya, Mahānāma, Assaji, Koṇḍañña
instances.recipient_features: the group-of-five being instructed in turns; each became a stream-enterer on successive days, then all attained arahatship at the close of the Anattalakkhaṇa-sutta
instances.occasion: the Buddha stayed inside the monastery to remove the impurities arising in each monk's meditation subject as they arose, going to each one's seat; on the fifth day he assembled them and taught the no-self discourse
instances.warrant: SN 22.59 Anattalakkhaṇa Sutta (the five monks' arahatship); MN 26 Pāsarāsi / Ariyapariyesanā frames the leading-in-practice
instances.evidence_pali: Uppanne uppanne kammaṭṭhānamale tepi bhikkhū bhagavato santikaṃ gantvā pucchanti. Bhagavāpi tesaṃ nisinnaṭṭhānaṃ gantvā malaṃ vinodeti.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (paṭikūlamanasikāra of the five skin-pentad parts: kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco)
instances.recipient: Dhammadinnā, the laywoman being ordained as a bhikkhunī
instances.recipient_features: former wife of Visākha; going forth out of faith (saddhāya pabbajati)
instances.occasion: at her ordination a learned/competent elder nun (byattā therī) recited the tacapañcaka to her, then shaved her head and ordained her
instances.warrant: the standard tacapañcaka (taco-pañcaka) given at the pabbajjā / ordination procedure (Vinaya Khandhaka); paṭikūlamanasikāra of MN 10 / DN 22 / Vism
instances.evidence_pali: Athekā byattā therī tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitvā kese ohāretvā pabbājesi.

instances.object: four objects: asubhānupassanā (kāye), āhāre paṭikūlasaññā, sabbaloke anabhiratisaññā, sabbasaṅkhāresu aniccānupassanā
instances.recipient: a class of monks (tepi bhikkhū) afflicted by rāga/dosa/moha
instances.recipient_features: monks whose minds are calmed of rāga/dosa/moha; the four objects are stated to quell and counter rāga, dosa, moha (rāgapaṭighātāni dosamohapaṭighātāni)
instances.occasion: the Buddha addresses the monks (Etha tumhe, bhikkhave...); they practise the four kammaṭṭhānas, develop insight, exhaust the āsavas, and reach arahatship
instances.warrant: the antidote saññā-cluster (asubha–āhāre paṭikūla–sabbaloke anabhirati–anicca); cf. the AN multi-saññā lists (e.g. AN 7.49 / AN 9.3 Meghiya); the rāga-dosa-moha-paṭighāta antidote matrix
instances.evidence_pali: Etha tumhe, bhikkhave, asubhānupassī kāye viharatha, āhāre paṭikūlasaññino sabbaloke anabhiratisaññino sabbasaṅkhāresu aniccānupassinoti. Imāni cattāri kammaṭṭhānāni kathesi.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (the five with skin: kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco; paṭikūlamanasikāra)
instances.recipient: Milakatthera, a former hunter, at his going-forth
instances.recipient_features: ex-hunter who made his living by killing (pāṇātipātakammena jīvikaṃ kappento); stirred to saṃvega (saṃviggacitto) by the water-pot omen, threw down his bow and asked to be ordained
instances.occasion: the saṃvigga ex-hunter asks an araññavāsi elder 'pabbājetha maṃ, bhante'; the elder tells him the tacapañcaka object and ordains him
instances.warrant: the standard tacapañcaka (dvattiṃsākāra subset) given at ordination per the Khandhaka ordination procedure (Vin Mahāvagga); cf. Khp 3 / Vism VIII paṭikūlamanasikāra
instances.evidence_pali: Thero tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitvā taṃ pabbājesi.

instances.object: dvattiṃsākāra-kammaṭṭhāna (the 32 parts; paṭikūlamanasikāra)
instances.recipient: Bhāradvāja (the king/brahmin interlocutor) whose lust-bound mind would not settle
instances.recipient_features: his mind would not enter/settle (cittaṃ anotarantaṃ) on the prior instruction to regard women as mother/sister/daughter; lust toward women to be bound
instances.occasion: the Buddha, seeing his mind not entering via that question, teaches the 32-part object by way of paṭikūlamanasikāra to bind/fix the mind
instances.warrant: dvattiṃsākāra / kāyagatāsati formula (DN 22 / MN 10 / MN 119); paṭikūlamanasikāra as asubha antidote to rāga (cf. AN 9.3)
instances.evidence_pali: Athassa tena pañhena cittaṃ anotarantaṃ disvā bhagavatā paṭikūlamanasikāravasena cittūpanibandhanatthaṃ vuttaṃ dvattiṃsākārakammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi.

instances.object: indriyasaṃvarasīla (sense-restraint), given after the asubha-kammaṭṭhāna proved too hard to develop
instances.recipient: the same recipient (the king/Bhāradvāja) of undeveloped sense-faculties
instances.recipient_features: abhāvitakāya (abhāvitapañcadvārikakāya — undeveloped in the five sense-doors); for such people the asubha meditation is hard to develop and the mind still would not enter
instances.occasion: seeing the mind still not entering even by this (asubha), the teacher teaches sense-faculty restraint, on which a bound mind cannot be harassed
instances.warrant: indriyasaṃvara as the standard graduated-training step (DN 2 / MN 27); adapting the object to capacity (indriya) when asubha is too hard for the abhāvitakāya — commentarial graduation
instances.evidence_pali: Itissa imināpi cittaṃ anotarantaṃ disvā indriyasaṃvarasīlaṃ kathesi.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna for the defilements destroyable by the three higher paths (uparimaggattayavajjhānaṃ kilesānaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ); specific subject unspecified
instances.recipient: a class of monks (te bhikkhū) who were already stream-enterers
instances.recipient_features: already attained sotāpattiphala; needing the object for the three remaining/higher paths
instances.occasion: the Buddha seats them ('etha, bhikkhave, nisīdatha') and declares he will teach the kammaṭṭhāna for the defilements to be cut by the three higher paths, then does so
instances.warrant: Samaya-sutta (SN 1.37) framing; the path-graded kilesa-pahāna scheme (defilements abandoned by each magga) and staged post-stream-entry vipassanā; no single canonical object named
instances.evidence_pali: Uparimaggattayavajjhānaṃ vo kilesānaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathessāmī’’ti kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi.

instances.object: pañcakkhandha-kammaṭṭhāna (the five aggregates as meditation subject)
instances.recipient: the elder/monk of the Ānandasutta (thera) who approached requesting it
instances.recipient_features: saw other monks given the five-aggregates kammaṭṭhāna, practise, attain arahatship and declare gnosis, and came wanting the same; the Buddha saw no abandoning of the higher-path defilements in him yet taught it to 'seize/mature his mind' (imassa cittaṃ gaṇhissāmi), making it a liberation-maturing application
instances.occasion: the monk approaches wishing for the same object others received; the Buddha teaches it to capture and ripen his mind even though he would not yet cut the higher-path defilements
instances.warrant: pañcakkhandha contemplation per the Khandhasaṃyutta (SN 22; the Ānandasutta SN 22.159 gives the aggregates as anicca/dukkha/anatta); vimuttiparipācanīyā dhammā (the cittaṃ-gaṇhana motive is the commentary's addition)
instances.evidence_pali: aññe bhikkhū pañcakkhandhakammaṭṭhānaṃ kathāpetvā yuñjitvā ghaṭetvā arahattaṃ patvā satthu santike aññaṃ byākaronte disvā

instances.object: asubha
instances.recipient: the Vesālī monks (a class of bhikkhus)
instances.recipient_features: attabhāve chandarāgena maraṇabhayabhītā — bound by desire-passion for the body, afraid of death, unable to purify their destiny
instances.occasion: the Buddha, foreseeing the monks cannot purify their gati while gripped by body-lust, resolves to teach asubha to remove their chanda-rāga so they win a heavenly rebirth, before withdrawing into a fortnight's seclusion
instances.warrant: SN 54.9 Vesālī-sutta (the Buddha teaches asubha then ānāpānasati to the Vesālī monks); the rāga→asubha antidote pairing (Meghiya formula AN 9.3 / Vism carita matrix)
instances.evidence_pali: handa nesaṃ chandarāgappahānāya asubhakathaṃ kathemi

instances.object: ānāpānassati-samādhi
instances.recipient: the monks (bhikkhū), addressed by the Buddha after they had earlier been given asubha-kammaṭṭhāna
instances.recipient_features: monks previously assigned asubha-kammaṭṭhāna before attaining arahatta; now given a different method
instances.occasion: after the asubha episode the Buddha addresses the monks and, replacing the earlier asubha subject, declares a different pariyāya — the ānāpānassati concentration, peaceful and sublime (santa, paṇīta)
instances.warrant: SN 54.9 Vesālī-sutta (the Buddha turns the saṅgha to ānāpānassati-samādhi after the asubha deaths); ānāpānasati as the santa-vihāra (SN 54 Ānāpānasaṃyutta)
instances.evidence_pali: bhikkhūnaṃ arahattappattiyā pubbe ācikkhitaasubhakammaṭṭhānato aññaṃ pariyāyaṃ ācikkhanto ānāpānassatisamādhītiādimāha

instances.object: dvattiṃsākāra (32 bodily parts / paṭikūlamanasikāra)
instances.recipient: Vaṅgīsa (newly ordained under Nigrodhakappa, formerly a skull-tapping diviner)
instances.recipient_features: a former diviner (chavasīsamantika) who sought to learn the Buddha's 'mantra'/craft and ordained to obtain it
instances.occasion: after ordination Vaṅgīsa asks for the 'sippa'; the Teacher tells him it has a preliminary work and then assigns him the 32-parts subject, which he attends to forward and backward, develops insight, and attains arahatship
instances.warrant: SN 8 Vaṅgīsa-saṃyutta ordination narrative; the dvattiṃsākāra / kāyagatāsati paṭikūlamanasikāra (DN 22 / MN 10 / Khp 3)
instances.evidence_pali: Athassa satthā dvattiṃsākārakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi. So taṃ anulomapaṭilomaṃ manasikaronto vipassanaṃ vaḍḍhetvā anukkamena arahattaṃ pāpuṇi

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (the five-skin pentad: hair/body-hair/nails/teeth/skin; paṭikūlamanasikāra)
instances.recipient: the ordinand at the moment of ordination (a class — every candidate at going-forth, shaving/bathing)
instances.recipient_features: may be one of 'pounded dispositions' (madditasaṅkhāra), developed-in-meditation (bhāvitabhāvana), ripe like a boil ready to burst — could reach arahatta at the razor's edge
instances.occasion: prescriptive ordination procedure: the preceptor, having told the ordinand to establish mindfulness, should declare the tacapañcaka subject, making its foulness/impurity and non-self plain
instances.warrant: Vinaya Mahāvagga ordination procedure (Mv I); the tacapañcaka / dvattiṃsākāra paṭikūlamanasikāra (DN 22 / Khp 3); Vism VIII
instances.evidence_pali: tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitabbaṃ, ācikkhantena ca vaṇṇasaṇṭhānagandhāsayokāsavasena asucijegucchapaṭikūlabhāvaṃ nijjīvanissattabhāvaṃ vā pākaṭaṃ karontena ācikkhitabbaṃ

instances.object: dvattiṃsākāra-kammaṭṭhāna (32 bodily parts / paṭikūlamanasikāra)
instances.recipient: the boy/novice (dāraka) ordained by the elder Siggava — the young Tissa (Moggaliputtatissa)
instances.recipient_features: a young brahmin boy newly ordained as a sāmaṇera; capable, reached stream-entry quickly; the elder deliberately withheld further development to keep him for learning the Buddha-word
instances.occasion: Tatiyasaṅgīti narrative: the elder ordains the boy and first assigns him the 32-parts subject; doing the preliminary work he is soon established in the fruit of stream-entry
instances.warrant: Tatiyasaṅgīti narrative (Dīpavaṃsa/Mahāvaṃsa; Sp Bāhiranidāna); the dvattiṃsākāra / kāyagatāsati paṭikūlamanasikāra (DN 22 / Khp 3)
instances.evidence_pali: Thero dārakaṃ pabbājetvā dvattiṃsākārakammaṭṭhānaṃ tāva ācikkhi. So tattha parikammaṃ karonto nacirasseva sotāpattiphale patiṭṭhahi

instances.object: asubha
instances.recipient: the monks (bhikkhū)
instances.recipient_features: addressed as a group, taught for their benefit/support (anuggahāya); no individual features stated
instances.occasion: the Buddha taught the asubha discourse to the monks for their benefit, framed under the heading of kammaṭṭhāna (not as praise of death), before withdrawing into a fortnight's seclusion
instances.warrant: Asubha-bhāvanā episode of the Vinaya Pārājika 3 nidāna (Vin III) / SN 54.9 Vesālī episode where the Buddha taught asubha then withdrew before the ānāpānassati substitution
instances.evidence_pali: Tato tesaṃ anuggahāya asubhakathaṃ kathesi kammaṭṭhānasīsena, no maraṇavaṇṇasaṃvaṇṇanādhippāyena.

instances.object: ānāpānassati
instances.recipient: the monks (bhikkhū)
instances.recipient_features: monks previously taught the asubha-kammaṭṭhāna, now addressed for the attainment of arahatta
instances.occasion: the Buddha, having earlier taught the asubha-kammaṭṭhāna, declares a different method (aññaṃ pariyāyaṃ) — ānāpānassati-samādhi — to the monks
instances.warrant: SN 54.9 (Vesālī episode where the Buddha substitutes ānāpānasati for asubha after the monks' deaths) / Vin III Pār 3 nidāna ānāpānassati origin
instances.evidence_pali: bhikkhūnaṃ arahattappattiyā pubbe ācikkhitaasubhakammaṭṭhānato aññaṃ pariyāyaṃ ācikkhanto ‘‘ānāpānassatisamādhī’’ti āha.

instances.object: asubha
instances.recipient: the addressee 'te' — one whose mind is inflamed/burning through perceptual distortion (saññā-vipariyesa), drawn to the beautiful sign bound up with lust
instances.recipient_features: mind burning from saññā-vipariyesa; drawn to a subha (beautiful) sign that is rāgūpasañhita (connected with lust)
instances.occasion: a quoted verse (SN 1.212 / Thag 1233-1234) directing the lust-afflicted person to avoid the beautiful sign and develop the mind with the unlovely (asubha)
instances.warrant: SN 1.212 / Thag 1233-1234 (the cited verse itself; the canonical rāga→asubha antidote)
instances.evidence_pali: Nimittaṃ parivajjehi, subhaṃ rāgūpasañhitaṃ; Asubhāya cittaṃ bhāvehi, ekaggaṃ susamāhitaṃ.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (meditation subject — unspecified in the text)
instances.recipient: three monks who came to the elder
instances.recipient_features: each declared willingness to die / exert themselves utterly for the teacher's sake; the elder judged them capable (bhabba)
instances.occasion: three monks come to the elder and each pledge extreme self-surrender (one to leap into a precipice, one to grind his body to nothing, one to stop his breath unto death); the elder judges them capable and gives a meditation subject — an exemplum in the attaniyyātana (self-surrender) section
instances.warrant: none — illustrative commentarial narrative in the attaniyyātana section; no specific canonical sutta locus assigns the (unnamed) object. The quoted frame 'buddhassa vā bhagavato ācariyassa vā attānaṃ niyyātetvā' is the Vism self-surrender formula being illustrated, not a canonical object-assignment.
instances.evidence_pali: Thero bhabbāvatime bhikkhūti kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi. Te tassa ovāde ṭhatvā tayopi arahattaṃ pāpuṇiṃsū

instances.object: kāyagatāsati / paṭikūlamanasikāra (dvattiṃsākāra, 32 parts)
instances.recipient: an ādikammika (beginner) clansman who wishes to develop this kammaṭṭhāna
instances.recipient_features: ādikammika kulaputta (beginner clansman) wishing to cultivate the kāyagatāsati subject
instances.occasion: the beginner approaches a kalyāṇamitta (good friend / teacher) to take up the subject
instances.warrant: Kāyagatāsati-sutta MN 119; Mahāsatipaṭṭhāna paṭikūlamanasikāra DN 22 / MN 10 (32-part body contemplation)
instances.evidence_pali: Imaṃ pana kammaṭṭhānaṃ bhāvetukāmena ādikammikena kulaputtena vuttappakāraṃ kalyāṇamittaṃ upasaṅkamitvā idaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetabbaṃ. Tenāpissa kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathentena sattadhā uggahakosallaṃ dasadhā ca manasikārakosallaṃ ācikkhitabbaṃ.

instances.object: paṭikūlamanasikāra / dvattiṃsākāra-pāḷi (32-part recitation)
instances.recipient: two elders (dve therā) who took the subject from Malayavāsī Mahādeva Thera
instances.recipient_features: monks already versed in two or three nikāyas; padakkhiṇaggāhitā (apt at grasping) — learners for whom recitation makes the subject clear
instances.occasion: the two elders requested the kammaṭṭhāna; the thera gave them the 32-part text to recite for four months, after which they became stream-enterers
instances.warrant: Kāyagatāsati-sutta MN 119 / paṭikūlamanasikāra DN 22 (32-part body recollection)
instances.evidence_pali: Thero kira tehi kammaṭṭhānaṃ yācito cattāro māse imaṃyeva sajjhāyaṃ karothāti dvattiṃsākārapāḷiṃ adāsi.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (unspecified vipassanā subject given to a dosacarita monk)
instances.recipient: a thera of hateful temperament (the puthujjana elder) supported by the monk Dhammadinna
instances.recipient_features: dosacarito (hateful temperament); still a worldling (puthujjana), shaken by the obhāsa (light) imperfection of insight
instances.occasion: the elder, recognizing he was still a worldling, asks Dhammadinna for support; Dhammadinna tells him the subject and the thera, taking it up on the walking path, attains arahantship at the third step
instances.warrant: none (commentarial narrative exemplum within the vipassanupakkilesa discussion)
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘Bhante, tumhākaṃ avassayo bhavissāmiccevāhaṃ āgato, mā cintayitthā’’ti kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi. Thero kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā caṅkamaṃ āruyha tatiye padavāre aggaphalaṃ arahattaṃ pāpuṇi. Thero kira dosacarito ahosi.

instances.object: mettā
instances.recipient: a monk (bhikkhu)
instances.recipient_features: addressed in 2nd person as 'bhikkhu' in the ovāda; one whose base concentration (mūlasamādhi / cittekaggatāmatta) is already established
instances.occasion: after the basic one-pointed concentration is established by the ovāda, the Buddha leads him not to rest content but to develop and expand that same samādhi via mettā cetovimutti, then the other vihāras (savitakka-savicāra … upekkhāsahagata)
instances.warrant: Meghiya-type instruction to a monk (cf. AN 9.3 / Ud 4.1; the 'mettā me cetovimutti bhāvitā' formula): establish the mind, then develop mettā cetovimutti, then expand the base samādhi savitakka-savicāra etc. — the quoted canonical formula the commentary glosses
instances.evidence_pali: Tato te bhikkhu evaṃ sikkhitabbaṃ mettā me cetovimutti bhāvitā bhavissati bahulīkatā yānikatā vatthukatā anuṭṭhitā paricitā susamāraddhāti

instances.object: asubha (rāgacarita); ānāpānasati (mohacarita/vitakkacarita); the six recollections incl. buddhānussati (saddhācarita)
instances.recipient: persons by temperament — rāgacarita, mohacarita, vitakkacarita, saddhācarita
instances.recipient_features: rāgabahula (lust-dominant); confusion-, discursive-, and faith-temperament types
instances.occasion: the sub-commentary expounding which kammaṭṭhāna suits each temperament (caritānukūlakammaṭṭhāna)
instances.warrant: Visuddhimagga III carita matrix; Niddesa carita typology; AN 9.3 (Meghiya antidote set)
instances.evidence_pali: rāgassa ujuvipaccanīkabhāvato asubhakammaṭṭhānaṃ tassa sappāyaṃ. Ānāpānaṃ mohacaritassa, vitakkacaritassa ca sappāyaṃ

instances.object: maraṇasati, upasamānussati, vavatthāna (dhātuvavatthāna)
instances.recipient: buddhicarita (intelligence-temperament person)
instances.recipient_features: buddhicarita — suited to profound objects (gambhīrabhāvato), the province of intelligence alone
instances.occasion: assigning the objects suitable to the intelligence-temperament
instances.warrant: Visuddhimagga III carita matrix; maraṇasati suttas
instances.evidence_pali: Maraṇaupasamasaññāvavatthānāni buddhicaritassa sappāyāni gambhīrabhāvato buddhiyā eva visayattā.

instances.object: kasiṇa — puthula (wide) for mohacarita; khuddaka (small) for vitakkacarita
instances.recipient: mohacarita and vitakkacarita persons
instances.recipient_features: confusion-temperament (scatters/becomes more confused in a cramped space); discursive-temperament (mind runs after a large object)
instances.occasion: assigning the suitable size of kasiṇa by temperament
instances.warrant: Visuddhimagga III–IV kasiṇa-size suitability by temperament
instances.evidence_pali: Puthulaṃ mohacaritassa sappāyaṃ sambādhe okāse cittassa bhiyyosomattāya sammuyhanato. Khuddakaṃ vitakkacaritassa sappāyaṃ mahantārammaṇassa vitakkasandhāvanapaccayattā.

instances.object: ten asubha + kāyagatāsati (rāgacarita); four brahmavihāra/appamaññā + four vaṇṇakasiṇa (dosacarita)
instances.recipient: rāgacarita and dosacarita persons
instances.recipient_features: lust-temperament (the eleven rāga-suppressing objects); hate-temperament (the eight dosa-suppressing objects)
instances.occasion: expounding the temperament-suitable kammaṭṭhāna matrix (taṃtaṃcaritānukūlakammaṭṭhānaṃ dassetvā)
instances.warrant: Visuddhimagga III–IV carita-suitability matrix; Niddesa carita typology
instances.evidence_pali: ime ekādasa rāgavikkhambhanassa upāyabhāvato rāgacaritassa anukūlā

instances.object: ānāpānasati (one recollection — anussati ekaka)
instances.recipient: mohacarita and vitakkacarita persons
instances.recipient_features: confusion- and discursive-temperament — mind steadied by the strength of counting (gaṇanabala)
instances.occasion: clarifying that the single object suits the moha/vitakka types
instances.warrant: Visuddhimagga III carita-suitability of ānāpānasati
instances.evidence_pali: Taṃ pana mohacaritassa, vitakkacaritassa ca gaṇanabalena cittassa patiṭṭhānato anukūlaṃ.

instances.object: the six recollections (saddhācarita); maraṇānussati/upasamānussati, āhāre paṭikkūlasaññā, catudhātuvavatthāna (buddhicarita); the remaining kasiṇas suit all temperaments
instances.recipient: saddhācarita, buddhicarita, and all temperament types
instances.recipient_features: faith-temperament (the first six recollections atisappāya); intelligence-temperament (death/peace recollection, food-repulsiveness, element-analysis); remaining kasiṇas have no temperament-specific bar
instances.occasion: completing the temperament-suitability matrix of the objects
instances.warrant: Visuddhimagga III carita-suitability matrix of the forty kammaṭṭhāna
instances.evidence_pali: purimaṃ anussatichakkaṃ saddhācaritassa atisappāyavasena anukūlaṃ

instances.object: the four dhammas to Meghiya (asubha/mettā/ānāpānasati/aniccasaññā); the seven kammaṭṭhānas incl. mettā to Rāhula
instances.recipient: Venerable Meghiya; Rāhula thera (named elders)
instances.recipient_features: Meghiya does not have all four caritas; Rāhula does not have all caritas — the full set was given to each despite this
instances.occasion: the sub-commentary reconciling why the Buddha gave a single named monk multiple objects, against the carita scheme; conclusion: do not fixate on the bare words but seek the intent
instances.warrant: AN 9.3 / Ud 4.1 (Meghiya); MN 62 Mahārāhulovāda (mettā etc. to Rāhula)
instances.evidence_pali: Tathā rāhulovādasuttepi ‘‘mettaṃ, rāhula, bhāvanaṃ bhāvehī’’tiādinā (ma. ni. 2.120) satta kammaṭṭhānāni vuttāni, na cāyasmato meghiyassa cattāripi caritāni santi, nāpi rāhulattherassa sabbacaritāni

instances.object: a clean cloth-rag (suddha coḷakhaṇḍa / rajoharaṇa) yielding aniccasaññā
instances.recipient: Cūḷapanthaka (named elder/monk)
instances.recipient_features: born dull (dandho jāto) from past kamma of mocking a slow monk; loses each line of text as he learns the next; had in a past life been a king who soiled a clean cloth wiping sweat
instances.occasion: the Buddha, seeing his past cause (pubbahetu), sets him to a fitting kammaṭṭhāna and gives him a clean cloth-rag
instances.warrant: Cūḷapanthaka narrative (Dhp-a / Thag-a; rajoharaṇa episode) — commentarial elaboration, no single canonical antidote-formula
instances.evidence_pali: tassa pubbahetuṃ disvā tadanurūpe kammaṭṭhāne niyojento suddhaṃ coḷakhaṇḍaṃ adāsi

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (vipassanā subject re-tuned/purified for indriya-balancing; specific subject unnamed)
instances.recipient: the elder Vakkali
instances.recipient_features: saddhā-dominant; faith so strong he could not enter the vipassanā path (saddhāya pana balavabhāvena vipassanāvīthiṃ na otari)
instances.occasion: after the verse and 'ehi vakkalī', he set up insight but faith overpowered it; the Buddha, knowing this, re-tuned and gave the subject
instances.warrant: Vakkali narrative SN 22.87 (Vakkalisutta); indriya-samatta / faculty-balancing doctrine (Vism IV indriyānaṃ samatā; Indriya-saṃyutta)
instances.evidence_pali: Taṃ ñatvā bhagavā tassa indriyasamattappaṭipādanāya kammaṭṭhānaṃ sodhetvā adāsi.

instances.object: other (Gotamaka-sutta taught to ripen insight; no single named kammaṭṭhāna)
instances.recipient: the five hundred monks (formerly conceited, now humbled and devoted to their supportive kammaṭṭhāna)
instances.recipient_features: nihatamānā, attano upakāra-kammaṭṭhāna-parāyaṇā — conceit subdued, intent on their helpful meditation subject; knowledge ripened
instances.occasion: the Buddha, wandering to Vesālī and dwelling at the Gotamaka cetiya, perceived the ripeness of the 500 monks' knowledge and spoke the sutta
instances.warrant: Gotamakacetiya-sutta (AN 3.123/3.126); teaching timed to ripened faculties (Buddha-perceives-ripeness formula)
instances.evidence_pali: imesaṃ pañcasatānaṃ bhikkhūnaṃ ñāṇaparipākaṃ viditvā idaṃ gotamakasuttaṃ kathesi

instances.object: carita-matrix: asubha (rāga); ānāpānassati (moha); the six recollections, esp. buddhānussati (saddhā); maraṇassati, upasamānussati, catudhātuvavatthāna, āhāre paṭikūlasaññā (ñāṇa/buddhi-carita)
instances.recipient: persons classed by temperament (rāga-, moha-, saddhā-, ñāṇa/buddhi-carita puggala)
instances.recipient_features: puggalavemattatā — the teacher discerns each person's individual disposition and gives the matching object accordingly
instances.occasion: general exposition that the teacher, knowing the variety of persons, gives (deti) to each the object that fits
instances.warrant: carita-kammaṭṭhāna suitability matrix, Vism III (cariyā-niddesa; carita-vasena kammaṭṭhāna-sappāya); Dasuttara/Niddesa carita classification
instances.evidence_pali: Tatthāpi puggalavemattataṃ ñatvā tadanurūpaṃ tadanurūpameva deti.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (vipassanā subject re-tuned/purified for indriya-balancing; specific subject unnamed)
instances.recipient: the elder Vakkali
instances.recipient_features: saddhā-dominant; faith too strong to enter the insight path (Saddhāya balavabhāvato vipassanāvīthiṃ na otarati)
instances.occasion: after the verse and 'ehi vakkalī', he set up insight but faith overpowered it; the Buddha, knowing this, re-tuned and gave the subject
instances.warrant: Vakkali narrative SN 22.87 (Vakkalisutta); indriya-samatta / faculty-balancing doctrine (Vism IV indriyānaṃ samatā)
instances.evidence_pali: taṃ ñatvā bhagavā tassa indriyasamattapaṭipādanāya kammaṭṭhānaṃ sodhetvā adāsi.

instances.object: asubha (and other carita-suited objects) withheld in favour of insight-talk; for the rāgacarita, bare vipassanā-first is unsuitable until the danger of sensuality (ādīnava) is shown
instances.recipient: the lustful-temperament person (rāgacarito puggalo)
instances.recipient_features: rāga-natured with disposition not yet purified (āsayassa asodhitattā), so direct insight-talk at the outset brings no distinction
instances.occasion: Netti Yuttihāra exposition: a teaching given against type yields no distinction (na visesāvahā)
instances.warrant: Nettippakaraṇa Yuttihāra (carita-sappāya / yutti principle); Vism III carita-vasena desanā-yutti
instances.evidence_pali: Rāgacaritassa tathā tathā kāmānaṃ ādīnavaṃ, okāraṃ, saṃkilesaṃ, nekkhamme ānisaṃsañca avibhāvetvā ādito vipassanākathāva kariyamānā na visesāvahā hoti āsayassa asodhitattāti

instances.object: aniccānupassanā (rāgacarita); dukkhānupassanā (dosacarita); anattānupassanā (mohacarita)
instances.recipient: persons by temperament (rāga-, dosa-, moha-carita)
instances.recipient_features: each contemplation declared suitable (sappāyā) so the matching carita's defilement finds no foothold (na ... patiṭṭhaṃ labhati)
instances.occasion: Netti Vicayahāra exposition pairing the three contemplations with the three temperaments
instances.warrant: three anupassanā ↔ carita pairing (Paṭisambhidāmagga; Netti Vicayahāra); rāga-anicca, dosa-dukkha, moha-anatta correspondence (cf. Vism)
instances.evidence_pali: aniccānupassanā rāgacaritassa sappāyā vuttā

instances.object: asubha (rāgacarita); mettā (dosacarita); paṭiccasamuppāda (mohacarita)
instances.recipient: the lustful-, hating-, and deluded-temperament persons (rāga-/dosa-/moha-carita puggala)
instances.recipient_features: asubha is repulsive, fitting rāga-removal; mettā opposes dosa; paṭiccasamuppāda's object is wisdom, fitting moha-removal (tibbakilesa rāgacarita noted)
instances.occasion: Netti Yuttihāra vibhāvanā — 'yathā hi bhagavā…' illustrating how the Blessed One fittingly teaches per type to remove each root (rāga/dosa/moha-vinaya)
instances.warrant: carita-antidote matrix (Vism III.74ff: asubha→rāga, mettā→dosa); Meghiya antidote AN 9.3; Nettippakaraṇa Yuttihāra
instances.evidence_pali: rāgacaritassa puggalassa asubhadesanā rāgavinayāya yuttā. Mettāya dosapaṭipakkhattā dosacaritassa puggalassa mettādesanā dosavinayāya yuttā. Paṭiccasamuppādassa paññāvisayattā mohacaritassa puggalassa paṭiccasamuppādadesanā mohavinayāya yuttā.

instances.object: the four brahmavihāras: mettā (vs byāpāda), karuṇā (vs vihesā), muditā (vs arati), upekkhā (vs rāga)
instances.recipient: the dweller in / attainer of each brahmavihāra (mettā-/karuṇā-/muditā-/upekkhā-vihārī puggala)
instances.recipient_features: for one dwelling in mettā, ill-will is abandoned and cannot overpower the mind; likewise each abode opposes its specific defilement
instances.occasion: Netti Yuttihāra vibhāvanā — gathering yutti via the brahmavihāra phala-samāpatti / vasībhāva, matching each abode to its antidote function
instances.warrant: brahmavihāra-paccanīka / hindrance-opposition formula (Vism IX: mettā↔byāpāda, karuṇā↔vihiṃsā, muditā↔arati, upekkhā↔rāga); Netti Yuttihāra
instances.evidence_pali: mettāvihārissa sato byāpādo pahānaṃ abbhatthaṃ gacchatīti desanā yujjati

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna purified/re-tuned for balancing the faculties (indriya-samatta); vipassanā object re-set after over-strong faith stalled progress
instances.recipient: the elder Vakkali
instances.recipient_features: saddhā (faith) faculty over-dominant (saddhāya balavabhāvato), so the faculties are unbalanced and he cannot descend into the vipassanā path
instances.occasion: after the Buddha spoke the verse and said 'ehi Vakkali'; elated, Vakkali set up insight but could not enter the insight-stream because faith was too strong
instances.warrant: Vakkali narrative (SN 22.87 Vakkalisutta); indriya-samatta / five-faculties balancing doctrine (cf. Vism IV on balancing saddhā↔paññā, vīriya↔samādhi)
instances.evidence_pali: Taṃ ñatvā bhagavā tassa indriyasamattapaṭipādanāya kammaṭṭhānaṃ sodhetvā adāsi. So satthārā dinnanayena vipassanaṃ ussukkāpetvā maggapaṭipāṭiyā arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna purified/re-tuned for balancing the faculties (indriya-samatta); vipassanā object re-set after predominant faith stalled progress
instances.recipient: the elder Vakkali
instances.recipient_features: saddhā (faith) faculty over-abundant (saddhāya bahulabhāvato); the remaining faculties not doing their work, so he cannot ascend the vipassanā path
instances.occasion: after the verse and 'ehi Vakkali'; Vakkali set up insight but could not climb the insight-path because of excess faith
instances.warrant: Vakkali narrative (SN 22.87 Vakkalisutta); indriya-samatta doctrine (five faculties; Vism IV). Row glosses 'etthāti saddhindriyassa adhimattabhāve sesindriyānaṃ sakiccākaraṇe' — faith-faculty in excess, the rest not doing their work.
instances.evidence_pali: Taṃ ñatvā bhagavā indriyasamattapaṭipādanāya kammaṭṭhānaṃ sodhetvā adāsi. So satthārā dinnanayena vipassanaṃ ussukkāpetvā maggapaṭipāṭiyā arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.

instances.object: the very door (mukha) of vipassanā-approach by which the monk had himself attained — taught back to him as phassāyatana-kammaṭṭhāna / pañcakkhandha-kammaṭṭhāna
instances.recipient: a monk asking for a meditation subject (kammaṭṭhānaṃ pucchanto bhikkhu)
instances.recipient_features: inquiring monk who had already established insight via a particular door (adhigatamagga); the teacher returns to him the very door by which he made his vipassanā-approach
instances.occasion: the monk asks for a kammaṭṭhāna; the teacher teaches him the same door (sense-base / five-aggregates contemplation) by which the insight-approach was made, noting the two do not agree with each other
instances.warrant: Kiṃsukopamasutta (SN 35.245); the six sense-bases as a city, teaching by the practitioner's own door of approach
instances.evidence_pali: Adhigatamaggameva kathesīti yena mukhena vipassanābhinivesaṃ akāsi, tamevassa mukhaṃ kathesi. Ayaṃ panāti kammaṭṭhānaṃ pucchanto bhikkhu.

instances.object: ānāpānassati (taught after, and in place of, the asubha kammaṭṭhāna)
instances.recipient: the assembled monks who had previously been taught the asubha kammaṭṭhāna
instances.recipient_features: a group of monks; for some it led to arahatta, for others to anāgāmi/sakadāgāmi/sotāpatti-fruit, for others to first jhāna or a good rebirth-support
instances.occasion: after the asubha practice and Ānanda's request for another method, the Buddha rebuts the misconception that the asubha teaching caused the monks' deaths and teaches another way (ānāpānassati)
instances.warrant: SN 54.9 / Vin Pār 3 Vesālī asubha → ānāpānassati substitution episode
instances.evidence_pali: idaṃ pana kammaṭṭhānaṃ tesaṃ kesañci arahattappattiyā, kesañci anāgāmisakadāgāmisotāpattiphalappattiyā, kesañci paṭhamajjhānādhigamāya

instances.object: dvattiṃsākāra-kammaṭṭhāna (32-part body contemplation / paṭikūlamanasikāra)
instances.recipient: the young brahmin (māṇava) who could not resolve the riddle-questions
instances.recipient_features: a māṇava reduced to distress (vighātaṃ pāpuṇi), unable to construe the questions up or down
instances.occasion: the teacher first taught him the 32-part kammaṭṭhāna 'as the preliminary approach to the mantra', leading to stream-entry; later the object is developed (vaḍḍhetvā) for the higher path
instances.warrant: dvattiṃsākāra / kāyagatāsati paṭikūlamanasikāra formula (DN 22 / MN 10 / Khp 3); Tatiyasaṅgīti narrative (Moggaliputtatissa and the māṇava)
instances.evidence_pali: Dvattiṃsākārakammaṭṭhānaṃ tāva ācikkhīti ‘‘atthi imasmiṃ kāye’’tiādikaṃ dvattiṃsākārakammaṭṭhānaṃ ‘‘mantassa upacāro aya’’nti paṭhamaṃ ācikkhi

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (the skin-pentad: kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco; paṭikūlamanasikāra)
instances.recipient: the ordinand at the moment of ordination / head-and-beard removal
instances.recipient_features: if madditasaṅkhāra / bhāvitabhāvana (ripe, like a boil ready to burst), can reach arahatta 'at the razor's edge' (khuragge)
instances.occasion: at ordination / head-shaving the preceptor says 'āvuso, suṭṭhu upadhārehi, satiṃ upaṭṭhāpehi' and must teach the tacapañcaka, expounding its loathsome/non-self nature
instances.warrant: tacapañcaka (kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco) paṭikūlamanasikāra formula (Khp 3 / DN 22); ordination-vinaya preceptor's first kammaṭṭhāna
instances.evidence_pali: atha khvassa ‘‘āvuso, suṭṭhu upadhārehi, satiṃ upaṭṭhāpehī’’ti vatvā tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitabbaṃ

instances.object: ānāpānassati (restated among the recollections as the subject suitable for the gathered monks)
instances.recipient: the assembled monks (tesaṃ bhikkhūnaṃ)
instances.recipient_features: many of the gathered monks for whom ānāpānassati is suitable and beneficial (bahūnaṃ sappāyā sātthikā)
instances.occasion: explaining why ānāpānassati is named separately though already covered by the ten recollections — because the Buddha taught that very kammaṭṭhāna to those monks; the four āruppas are excluded as unsuited to beginners
instances.warrant: the sappāya-kammaṭṭhāna / dasānussati framework (SN 54 ānāpānasaṃyutta; Vism III)
instances.evidence_pali: Tathā hi bhagavā tameva kammaṭṭhānaṃ tesaṃ bhikkhūnaṃ kathesi.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (unspecified meditation subject) given at ordination
instances.recipient: Upaka (the Ājīvaka who sought 'Anantajina'), a named person
instances.recipient_features: an older man (mahallako jāto) gone forth late, from Vaṅkahāra-janapada
instances.occasion: after seeking out the Buddha and being ordained, the Buddha gives him a kammaṭṭhāna; working at it he reaches anāgāmi-phala, is reborn in Aviha, then attains arahatta
instances.warrant: MN 26 / Vin Mahāvagga Upaka encounter; the seven reborn in Aviha who attained arahatta (cf. SN 1.50 Ghaṭīkāra)
instances.evidence_pali: Bhagavā pabbājetvā tassa kammaṭṭhānaṃ adāsi. So kammaṭṭhāne kammaṃ karonto anāgāmiphale patiṭṭhāya

instances.object: ānāpānassati paṭibhāga-nimitta (counterpart-sign phase)
instances.recipient: the meditator engaged in the practice (bhāvanamanuyutta) who has obtained the counterpart-sign
instances.recipient_features: liable to slacken into complacency (vosāna) or to despond (visāda) over the nimitta
instances.occasion: when the counterpart-sign arises, the Kakacūpama directive on what to tell him — 'punappunaṃ evaṃ manasi karohi' — so he neither slackens nor despairs but redoubles effort and steadies the mind on the sign
instances.warrant: MN 21 Kakacūpama (cited); Vism VIII ānāpānassati nimitta-handling; AN 9.1 / Ud 31 ānāpānassati vitakkupacchedāya
instances.evidence_pali: tasmā ‘‘punappunaṃ evaṃ manasi karohī’’ti vattabbo

instances.object: the four brahmavihāras (mettā, karuṇā, muditā, upekkhā)
instances.recipient: people of the human world facing the destruction of the eon (a class)
instances.recipient_features: ordinary beings in the human realm a hundred-thousand years before the kappa's end; some famine-stricken, some incapable of jhāna-attainment
instances.occasion: at the impending eon-destruction the lokabyūhā devas roam the human realm proclaiming the coming end and urge all to develop the brahmavihāras (and honour parents/elders) so as to gain jhāna and brahma-rebirth
instances.warrant: AN 7.66 Sattasūriya (seven-suns / kappa-destruction) + the brahmavihāra → brahmaloka rebirth formula (DN 13 Tevijja)
instances.evidence_pali: mettaṃ mārisā bhāvetha, karuṇaṃ, muditaṃ, upekkhaṃ mārisā bhāvetha

instances.object: unstated kammaṭṭhāna (subject not named) — given to restore a fallen elder
instances.recipient: Uccavālaṅkavāsī Mahānāga-thera, a long-ordained elder who had mistaken his attainment and was still a puthujjana
instances.recipient_features: dosacarito ahosi; thought himself an arahant but startled into fear (sārajja) by the conjured charging elephant, proving he was no khīṇāsava
instances.occasion: His pupil Dhammadinna-thera flies to him, exposes by a conjured elephant that he is still a worldling, then sits at his feet and gives him a kammaṭṭhāna; the elder attains arahatta on the third step of the walking-path
instances.warrant: carita-matrix antidote frame (dosacarita; cf. Vism I.74ff / Niddesa carita scheme) underlies the dosacarito note, but no specific canonical narrative — commentarial elaboration with an unstated object
instances.evidence_pali: kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi. Thero kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā caṅkamaṃ oruyha tatiye padavāre aggaphalaṃ arahattaṃ pāpuṇi. Thero kira dosacarito ahosi.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (the five-fold skin meditation / paṭikūlamanasikāra on kesa-loma-nakha-danta-taco)
instances.recipient: Dabba-kumāra (Dabba the Mallian), a seven-year-old boy being ordained
instances.recipient_features: sattavassikova (only seven years old); pubbahetusampanno, katābhinīhāro; gained saṃvega on seeing the Buddha
instances.occasion: At his ordination the Buddha directs a monk to ordain him; the ordaining monk teaches the tacapañcaka during the head-shaving, and the boy attains the four fruits as the hair falls
instances.warrant: Standard Vinaya/ordination tacapañcaka procedure at pabbajjā (kesāpanayana); the khuragge-arahatta formula; Vism VIII kāyagatāsati / dvattiṃsākāra
instances.evidence_pali: So thero satthu vacanaṃ sutvā dabbakumāraṃ pabbājento tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi.

instances.object: vīṇopama (the lute simile — balanced effort, tuning the strings neither too taut nor too slack)
instances.recipient: Soṇa (Soṇa Koḷivisa), a monk expert in the musician's craft
instances.recipient_features: gandhabbasippe cheko, ciṇṇavasī; will quickly recognize a teaching set in his own domain
instances.occasion: The Buddha judges that ordinary kammaṭṭhāna is unneeded for this monk and chooses the lute simile precisely because Soṇa is a master musician who will catch on at once
instances.warrant: Soṇa / Vīṇopama-sutta (AN 6.55) — the Buddha's lute simile on balanced effort, with the commentary framing the canonical teaching as fitted to the recipient's prior skill
instances.evidence_pali: satthā ‘‘imassa bhikkhuno avasesakammaṭṭhānena attho natthi, ayaṃ gandhabbasippe cheko ciṇṇavasī, attano visaye kathiyamāne khippameva sallakkhessatī’’ti vīṇopamaṃ kathetuṃ

instances.object: balancing of effort with samatha and equalizing the indriyas, then grasping the nimitta (samatha-/vipassanā-/magga-/phala-nimitta)
instances.recipient: Soṇa (Soṇa Koḷivisa), the over-strenuous monk
instances.recipient_features: effort too taut (accāraddha → uddhacca) then too slack (atilīna → kosajja); needs the faculties balanced
instances.occasion: After the lute simile the Buddha instructs Soṇa to yoke energy to calm, balance the faculties, and grasp the nimitta — placing him in arahatta and teaching the kammaṭṭhāna
instances.warrant: AN 6.55 (Soṇa / Vīṇopama-sutta) on tuning effort like a lute-string; Vism IV indriya-samatta / visuddhi. 1.60-62 cited in the text for the faculty-balancing schema
instances.evidence_pali: taṃ samathanimittaṃ vipassanānimittaṃ magganimittaṃ phalanimittañca gaṇha nibbattehīti evamassa satthā arahatte pakkhipitvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi.

instances.object: the carita matrix: asubha (rāgacarita), mettā (dosacarita), uddesa-paripucchā-dhammassavana-dhammasākacchā then ānāpānasati (mohacarita), ānāpānasati (vitakkacarita), the six anussati (saddhācarita), maraṇasati / upasamānussati / catudhātuvavatthāna / āhārepaṭikūlasaññā (ñāṇacarita)
instances.recipient: monks coming in groups before the rains for kammaṭṭhāna, sorted by temperament (a class)
instances.recipient_features: cariyavasena — rāgacarita, dosacarita, mohacarita, vitakkacarita, saddhācarita, ñāṇacarita
instances.occasion: Before the rains-retreat the monks gather and request kammaṭṭhāna ('kammaṭṭhānaṃ no dethā'); the Buddha assigns each the object suited to his temperament
instances.warrant: carita matrix of Vism III (cariyānurūpa kammaṭṭhāna) / Niddesa carita scheme; the standard temperament-to-object antidote mapping (asubha-for-rāga, mettā-for-dosa, ānāpānasati-for-vitakka; cf. Meghiya AN 9.3 / Ud 4.1)
instances.evidence_pali: Satthā tesaṃ cariyavasena rāgacaritassa asubhakammaṭṭhānaṃ deti, dosacaritassa mettākammaṭṭhānaṃ, mohacaritassa ‘‘uddeso paripucchā kālena dhammassavanaṃ kālena dhammasākacchā idaṃ tuyhaṃ sappāya’’nti ācikkhati.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (the five-fold skin meditation / paṭikūlamanasikāra), expounded by colour-shape-smell-seat-location and the lifeless/soulless nature
instances.recipient: the ordinand at pabbajjā (a class), given by the ordaining monk
instances.recipient_features: potentially upanissayasampanno — then attains arahatta during the very head-shaving
instances.occasion: The ordination rule that the ordaining monk must give the tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna, telling the candidate to attend well and establish mindfulness, expounding its repulsiveness/non-self before shaving
instances.warrant: commentarial / Khandhaka ordination procedure (aṭṭhakathā) prescribing tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna at kesāpanayana; the khuragge-arahatta formula; Vism VIII kāyagatāsati / dvattiṃsākāra
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘āvuso, suṭṭhu upadhārehi, satiṃ upaṭṭhāpehīti vatvā tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitabbaṃ.

instances.object: asubha (then vipassanā: anicca/dukkha/anatta on saṅkhāras)
instances.recipient: a person of lustful temperament (rāgavā), the addressee of the glossed verse
instances.recipient_features: rāga / kāmarāga to be suppressed (samatha) then cut off (vipassanā)
instances.occasion: Vism-mhṭ gloss on the verse-instruction to avoid the beauty-sign (subhanimitta), develop asubha, then see formations as anicca/dukkha/anatta and extinguish lust by the path
instances.warrant: asubha-for-rāga antidote (carita matrix, Vism III); cf. AN 9.3 Meghiya antidote-set (asubha to abandon rāga, aniccasaññā for asmimāna)
instances.evidence_pali: atha kho asubhabhāvanāya attano cittaṃ bhāvehi

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna purified/balanced and given (vipassanā subject; specific subject unstated)
instances.recipient: the elder Vakkali (Vakkalitthera)
instances.recipient_features: faith too strong (saddhāya balavabhāvato) so he could not enter the vipassanā stream; needed indriya-balancing
instances.occasion: after the Buddha spoke the verse and said 'ehi vakkalī'; Vakkali began vipassanā but his excess faith stalled it, so the Buddha, restoring balance of faculties, purified and gave him the subject — cited as the exemplar for indriya-samatā (the appanā-kosalla)
instances.warrant: Vakkali narrative (SN 22.87 Vakkalisutta; cf. Dhp-a verse 381); indriya-samatā as an appanā-kosalla, balancing of the five faculties (Vism IV; cf. AN 6.55 Soṇa faith↔energy balance)
instances.evidence_pali: Taṃ ñatvā bhagavā indriyasamataṃ paṭipādento kammaṭṭhānaṃ sodhetvā adāsi. So satthārā dinnanayena vipassanaṃ ussukkāpetvā maggapaṭipāṭiyā arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.

instances.object: vaṇṇakasiṇa (colour-kasiṇa on hair etc., kesādīsu vaṇṇakasiṇārammaṇāni)
instances.recipient: the person to whom hair etc. appear by colour (yassa vaṇṇato upaṭṭhāti kesādi, taṃ puggalaṃ)
instances.recipient_features: perceptual disposition: kesādi present themselves to him by colour rather than by repulsiveness/element
instances.occasion: Kāyagatāsati paṭikkūlamanasikāra-pabba exposition distinguishing paṭikkūla from dhātu, noting the jhānas with colour-kasiṇa objects are set out 'with reference to' such a person, who is to be ascertained before teaching (Ñatvā ācikkhitabba)
instances.warrant: Kāyagatāsati-sutta MN 119 (paṭikkūlamanasikāra pabba) + the carita-to-object matching principle / vaṇṇa-kasiṇa-from-the-body teaching (Vism VIII)
instances.evidence_pali: Yassa vaṇṇato upaṭṭhāti kesādi, taṃ puggalaṃ sandhāya jhānāni kesādīsu vaṇṇakasiṇārammaṇāni vibhattāni. Ñatvā ācikkhitabbanti

instances.object: other (an arahant elder conjures a frightening apparition — elephant etc. — via abhiññā-samādhi to reveal the elder's puthujjana status, intending afterward to re-yoke him to his kammaṭṭhāna)
instances.recipient: an elder of hateful temperament (dosacarita thera)
instances.recipient_features: dosacarita; bold/sūra yet quick to fear; khippavirāgī (quick to detach), khippaṃ paṭivijjhi (penetrated quickly)
instances.occasion: a khīṇāsava (arahant) elder, knowing the dosacarita elder's disposition, conjures an elephant to frighten him into realizing he is still a worldling, intending then to set him on the meditation subject
instances.warrant: the carita matrix (rāga/dosa/moha-carita typology of Vism Ch. III / the Niddesa) — specifically the dosacarita→khippavirāgī quick-penetration principle ('khippavirāgī hi dosacarito'); but the episode itself is a commentarial skilful-means anecdote with no direct sutta narrative warrant
instances.evidence_pali: Pacchā upāyena puthujjanabhāvaṃ viññāpetvā kammaṭṭhāne niyojessāmīti cintitattā vuttaṃ ‘‘avassayo bhavissāmiccevāhaṃ āgato’’ti

instances.object: the six recollections (buddhānussati, devatānussati, etc.)
instances.recipient: Mahānāma (the Sakyan)
instances.recipient_features: lay disciple living the crowded household life (puttasambādhasayanaṁ ajjhāvasanto); told to develop the recollections in every posture and amid household work
instances.occasion: Buddha instructs Mahānāma to sustain the recollections in any posture and circumstance
instances.warrant: self
instances.evidence_pali: Imaṁ kho tvaṁ, mahānāma, buddhānussatiṁ gacchantopi bhāveyyāsi, ṭhitopi bhāveyyāsi, nisinnopi bhāveyyāsi, sayānopi bhāveyyāsi, kammantaṁ adhiṭṭhahantopi bhāveyyāsi, puttasambādhasayanaṁ ajjhāvasantopi bhāveyyāsi.

instances.object: mettā
instances.recipient: bhikkhū
instances.recipient_features: monks who feel resentment (āghāta) toward a person; mettā prescribed as the antidote toward that person
instances.occasion: first discourse on dispelling resentment (āghātapaṭivinaya) — develop mettā toward the resented person
instances.warrant: self
instances.evidence_pali: mettā tasmiṁ puggale bhāvetabbā; evaṁ tasmiṁ puggale āghāto paṭivinetabbo.

instances.object: mettā (mettācetovimutti)
instances.recipient: bhikkhū (and, by extension, any woman or man)
instances.recipient_features: general assembly; said to be developable by woman or man alike (itthiyā vā purisena vā)
instances.occasion: Karajakāyasutta on the body born of action and the fruit of intentional deeds; mettācetovimutti
instances.warrant: self
instances.evidence_pali: “Bhāvetabbā kho panāyaṁ, bhikkhave, mettācetovimutti itthiyā vā purisena vā.

instances.object: asubha
instances.recipient: Abhirūpanandā (the therī)
instances.recipient_features: a nun renowned for her beauty/attachment to bodily form; the foulness antidote counters that attachment
instances.occasion: Buddha (per apadāna) instructing Abhirūpanandā to develop foulness-perception, unified and well-concentrated
instances.warrant: self
instances.evidence_pali: Asubhāya cittaṁ bhāvehi, ekaggaṁ susamāhitaṁ;

instances.object: asubha (with kāyagatāsati)
instances.recipient: Khemā (the therī)
instances.recipient_features: a nun noted for beauty; directed to develop foulness, body-mindfulness, and abundant disenchantment (nibbidā)
instances.occasion: Buddha (per apadāna) directing Khemā to develop foulness-perception, unified and concentrated, with body-directed mindfulness toward disenchantment
instances.warrant: self
instances.evidence_pali: Asubhāya cittaṁ bhāvehi, ekaggaṁ susamāhitaṁ; Sati kāyagatā tyatthu, nibbidā bahulā bhava.

instances.object: buddhānussati
instances.recipient: Subhūti (addressed in the Apadāna past-life birth-story frame)
instances.recipient_features: future Subhūti receiving a past-Buddha's/teacher's instruction in an Apadāna autobiographical past-life narrative
instances.occasion: past-life vow narrative in Subhūtittheraapadāna; a former Buddha/teacher instructing the future Subhūti
instances.warrant: self
instances.evidence_pali: ‘Bhāvehi buddhānussatiṁ, bhāvanānamanuttaraṁ; Imaṁ satiṁ bhāvayitvā, pūrayissasi mānasaṁ.

instances.object: asubha
instances.recipient: Nandā (Nandātherī)
instances.recipient_features: addressed by name (nande); beauty/conceit-prone, directed to contemplate the foul, diseased body and develop a one-pointed, well-concentrated mind
instances.occasion: Nandātherīapadāna — the Buddha/teacher directing Nandā to gaze on the foul decaying body and develop asubha to one-pointedness
instances.warrant: self
instances.evidence_pali: ‘Āturaṁ kuṇapaṁ pūtiṁ, passa nande samussayaṁ; Asubhāya cittaṁ bhāvehi, ekaggaṁ susamāhitaṁ.

instances.object: asubha
instances.recipient: Nanda (nande, vocative)
instances.recipient_features: addressed by name (passa nande samussayaṃ); the body shown as diseased, impure, foul
instances.occasion: Apadāna verse exhorting contemplation of the foul body
instances.warrant: self
instances.evidence_pali: Asubhāya cittaṃ bhāvehi, ekaggaṃ susamāhitaṃ

instances.object: asubha (with kāyagatāsati)
instances.recipient: āyasmā Vaṅgīsa
instances.recipient_features: a bhikkhu (Vaṅgīsa) beset by lustful thoughts (rāga); Ānanda counsels the asubha / mindfulness-of-body antidote, directing him to nibbidā
instances.occasion: Ānanda addresses Vaṅgīsa in verse, directing him to develop asubha and kāyagatāsati with one-pointed concentrated mind and become full of disenchantment
instances.warrant: self
instances.evidence_pali: Asubhāya cittaṁ bhāvehi, ekaggaṁ susamāhitaṁ; Sati kāyagatā tyatthu, nibbidābahulo bhava.

instances.object: ānāpānasati
instances.recipient: āyasmā Ariṭṭha
instances.recipient_features: a bhikkhu who reports he already develops ānāpānassati; the Buddha leads him to the detailed/complete account
instances.occasion: the Buddha asks the bhikkhū whether they develop ānāpānassati; Ariṭṭha replies, and the Buddha directs him to listen for its full development
instances.warrant: self
instances.evidence_pali: Api ca, ariṭṭha, yathā ānāpānassati vitthārena paripuṇṇā hoti taṁ suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.

instances.object: asubha (with kāyagatāsati)
instances.recipient: āyasmā Vaṅgīsa
instances.recipient_features: Vaṅgīsa, beset by lustful thoughts / sensual desire (rāga), directed (by Ānanda, within the Vaṅgīsa-thera collection) to develop the foul and mindfulness of the body and reach nibbidā
instances.occasion: verse directing Vaṅgīsa to develop asubha and kāyagatāsati, with one-pointed concentrated mind, and become full of disenchantment
instances.warrant: self
instances.evidence_pali: Asubhāya cittaṁ bhāvehi, ekaggaṁ susamāhitaṁ; Sati kāyagatā tyatthu, nibbidābahulo bhava.
